title: Markdown source view and edit feature request

turns.prompt: Add source view option to view and edit the markdown source

turns.content:
I’m loading the workspace instructions and the relevant editor code first, then I’ll trace how the current markdown editing UI is structured so I can add a source-edit mode without cutting across existing behavior.

I’m collecting the workspace bootstrap and identity files alongside the active editor implementation so the change lands in the right place and follows the repo’s conventions.

I’ve confirmed the editor only has WYSIWYG, split, and preview modes right now, and the active file already owns that mode switch. Next I’m reading the rest of the implementation and the surrounding save/position flow so I can add a true source editor mode instead of a superficial toggle.

The cleanest change here is a separate source mode backed by a plain EditText, while leaving the current WYSIWYG pipeline intact. I’m patching the editor to add that mode, reuse the existing cursor/scroll persistence, and keep the toolbar wired to whichever editable pane is active.

```

```

The source mode is in place in the editor component. I’m validating the Kotlin file now for type or import issues before I touch anything else, then I’ll decide whether a broader build check is warranted.

The file-level check is clean. I’m running a focused Gradle compile for the app module to make sure the refactor integrates with the Android build, since the existing terminal history shows this workspace has had build failures before.

The compile passed, so the change is structurally sound. I’m running the required code scan on the modified Android project now to catch any security regressions introduced by the new editor path before I close this out.

Added a new `Source` mode to the markdown editor so the raw markdown can be viewed and edited directly, without the inline WYSIWYG span rendering. The mode selector now includes `Source`, and that pane uses a plain `EditText` while preserving the existing cursor/scroll tracking and toolbar formatting behavior.

The change is in [e:\sentinel-editor\SentinelEditor\app\src\main\java\com\sentinel\editor\ui\RichMarkdownEditor.kt](/

). I refactored the editable pane into a shared editor path with two variants: plain source editing and the existing WYSIWYG rendering path.

Verification: `:app:compileDebugKotlin` passed, and the Snyk code scan for e:\sentinel-editor\SentinelEditor returned `0` issues.

1. If you want, I can extend this further with a `Source + Preview` split mode instead of keeping split as WYSIWYG + preview only.

toolCalls.name: execution_subagent
toolCalls.summary: Execution complete: Run focused Gradle compile

turns.content:
I’m checking the Android deployment path now: first whether a device is visible to `adb`, then why the prior `installDebug` failed, so I can either install the APK directly or tell you the exact blocker.

The app is installed on your phone. `adb` detected device `ASUS_I005DC`, and `:app:installDebug` completed successfully from e:\sentinel-editor\SentinelEditor.

If you want, I can also launch the app on the device and verify it opens cleanly.

toolCalls.name: execution_subagent
toolCalls.summary: Execution complete: Check device and install app

turns.content: I’m checking the working tree first so I can commit only the relevant changes and avoid sweeping in anything unrelated.
